data.title: Bank of America (BAC) Warns Investors Are Unprepared for a Stock Market Correction
data.description:
Bank of America (BAC) is warning that most investors are not prepared for a stock market correction after a three-year bull run in equities.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Analysts at the second-largest U.S. lender are basing their warning on the latest survey of fund managers. According to Bank of America, at the start of this year, U.S. fund managers were the most bullish since July 2021 while protection against a stock market correction had tumbled to an eight-year low.

The January survey also found that 38% of fund managers expected stronger global growth this year and sustained corporate earnings. At the same time, cash levels had fallen to a record low while exposure to stocks was at its highest level since December 2024, with 48% of managers overweight equities.

The latest survey results pushed Bank of America's Bull and Bear indicator to a "hyper-bull" level.

The Greenland Downturn

according to a survey from Bank of America Corp. conducted before the weekend's escalations over Greenland.

which indicates investors need to load up on risk hedges and safe havens. Instead, nearly half of participants said they do not have protection against a sharp fall in equity prices, the highest level since 2018.

"Low levels of stock market hedging are irrelevant in a world of positive surprises," Hartnett said. "But it matters greatly if surprises suddenly turn greatly."

Investors ran into one of those surprises over the weekend after the poll was conducted when President Donald Trump threatened new tariffs on eight European countries that have opposed his demand for Denmark to hand over Greenland to the US. The renewed rhetoric of a trade war dented risk appetite. European stocks slid over the past two days while US equity futures slide before cash trading resumes after a holiday on Monday.

The survey showed investors consider geopolitical conflict as the biggest risk to financial markets for the first time since October 2024, putting worries around a potential AI bubble in second place.

The BofA survey was conducted between Jan. 9 and Jan. 15, and canvassed 196 participants with $575 billion in assets under management.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Palantir Stock (PLTR) Slips despite Major Deal with South Korea's HD Hyundai
data.description:
Shares of AI software firm Palantir Technologies (PLTR) are down at the time of writing despite securing a major expansion deal with South Korea's HD Hyundai, according to Reuters. The agreement, which was confirmed by Palantir executives, will see the company provide additional software to the industrial giant over several years. Importantly, a source familiar with the matter said that the contract is worth hundreds of millions of dollars.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

It's worth noting that the new deal builds on a partnership that began in 2021, when HD Hyundai first started using Palantir's software to streamline its shipbuilding operations. Since then, the companies have said that the technology has helped HD Hyundai manufacture ships roughly 30% faster by improving coordination and operational efficiency. To formally mark the deal, the two companies planned a signing ceremony at Palantir's office space during the World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland.

Nevertheless, Palantir CEO Alex Karp made it clear that international growth is not the company's main priority. While he described South Korea as one of the most innovative and creative markets in the world, he said that Palantir is being selective about selling outside of the U.S. Indeed, Karp explained that demand in America remains so strong that the company can afford to focus primarily on its home market while pursuing overseas deals only when they are especially attractive.

Is PLTR Stock a Good Buy?

Overall, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on PLTR stock based on five Buys, 10 Holds, and two Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average PLTR price target of $192.88 per share implies 13.6% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Bristol Myers and Microsoft Partner to Accelerate AI Lung Cancer Detection
data.description:
Bristol Myers Squibb (BMY) and Microsoft (MSFT) have launched a new initiative to tackle lung cancer using artificial intelligence. The partnership will use Microsoft's Precision Imaging Network, a system already active in over 80% of U.S. hospitals, to help doctors find "hard-to-detect lung nodules" and diagnose patients much earlier than current methods allow.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The collaboration uses advanced technology to manage the entire clinical path, from the first scan to the final treatment plan. Microsoft's platform acts as an extra set of eyes for busy radiologists, flagging tiny abnormalities that might be overlooked during routine checkups. This is key because more than half of patients who have suspicious spots found on unrelated scans currently get lost to follow-up. To fix this, the new system includes automated tracking tools that ensure these high-risk patients are scheduled for necessary biopsies or specialist visits.

Whats Is the Impact on Bristol Myers?

Bristol Myers is using this tech to find more patients who can benefit from its flagship cancer treatments like Opdivo. While the stock dipped slightly by 0.45% in early trading today, the long-term outlook remains positive. Analyst David Risinger at Leerink and analyst Louise Chen at Scotiabank have recently raised their price targets for BMY to $60, noting that the company's "catalyst-rich" pipeline makes it a strong pick for 2026. Investors continue to favor the stock for its defensive qualities and its impressive dividend yield, which currently sits near 4.7%.

What Is the Impact on Microsoft Stock?

For Microsoft, this deal provides concrete proof that its massive investments in AI are paying off in the real world. By becoming the digital backbone for cancer detection, Microsoft is driving more traffic to its Azure cloud services. Wall Street is highly optimistic, with Goldman Sachs' Gabriela Borges setting a price target of $655 for the tech giant. Analysts believe that these commercial healthcare wins reinforce Microsoft's growth story and justify its high spending on new data centers.

Other Healthcare Firms Race for AI Tools

Bristol Myers and Microsoft are not the only players in this AI race. AstraZeneca (AZN) recently made its own move by acquiring the oncology AI startup Modella AI to speed up its clinical trials. Meanwhile, Merck (MRK) has partnered with Guardant Health to develop blood-based liquid biopsy tests. These overlapping strategies show that the world's biggest drugmakers are racing to own the diagnostic tools that tell doctors which pills to use.

Investors can compare the stocks mentioned in the article side-by-side on the TipRanks Stocks Comparison Tool. Click on the image below to explore the tool.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: 10-Year Treasury Yield Jumps to 4-Month High amid Japanese Bond Turmoil
data.description:
The 10-year yield is up by 4.4 bps to 4.275% on Tuesday, marking its highest level since September 2025. As a reminder, the yield on notes and bonds moves in the opposite direction of their prices.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

A major factor driving the 10-year yield higher is the selloff in Japanese bonds. Earlier today, the yield on Japan's 40-year bonds surpassed 4% for the first time after Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi teased tax cuts that could lead to inflation. Expectations of higher inflation tend to increase yields.

Why is the 10-Year Yield Up Today?

Furthermore, higher yields on Japanese bonds could attract capital away from foreign debt, such as U.S. Treasuries. "Yields on JGBs have reached levels that make investing in US Treasuries unattractive on a currency-hedged basis," said Lazard Asset Management Chief Market Strategist Ronald Temple. "If JGB yields continue to rise, a rational choice by Japanese investors could be to move capital back to Japan."

Bond traders are also digesting President Trump's new tariffs on eight European countries. Trump's economic policies, at times unpredictable, have led traders to question the appeal of U.S. Treasuries.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Palantir (PLTR) Stock Slips Below $170: This Analyst Sees 70% Downside Ahead
data.description:
Palantir (NASDAQ:PLTR) stock tumbled below the $170 mark today after failing to hold what many investors viewed as a key level. The pullback doesn't appear to be company-specific. Instead, investors are reacting to a broader risk-off move driven by renewed geopolitical tensions tied to President Donald Trump's comments regarding Greenland, which rattled global markets and sent growth stocks lower.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Those comments reopened a geopolitical fault line between the U.S. and Europe. Trump reiterated that Greenland holds strategic importance for U.S. national security, reviving fears that rhetoric could spill into concrete policy actions, including trade measures or diplomatic retaliation. European leaders pushed back forcefully, emphasizing Greenland's sovereignty and warning against any escalation. The uncertainty quickly filtered through markets, driving investors out of risk assets and into safer havens.

Meanwhile, RBC analyst Rishi Jaluria remains bearish on Palantir, reiterating an Underperform rating and a $50 price target, implying ~70% downside from current levels. (To watch Jaluria's track record, click here)

Jaluria argues that while Palantir's commercial business has shown signs of acceleration, much of that momentum may be driven by one-time factors rather than sustainable demand. The analyst believes competitive pressures are intensifying, particularly from large platform providers, and expects commercial growth to settle at far more modest levels over time.

The analyst is also skeptical about Palantir's ability to scale efficiently. According to Jaluria, the company's reliance on customized deployments and a consultative sales model limits its ability to penetrate smaller customers and restrains operating leverage. As he puts it, the approach "does not scale down-market," making it difficult to justify today's valuation.

On the government side, traditionally Palantir's strongest segment, Jaluria sees signs of deceleration as well, with deal values trending lower and growth slowing faster than anticipated. Until there's clear evidence of reacceleration, Jaluria believes the risk-reward remains skewed to the downside.

The rest of Wall Street, however, appears content to stay mostly on the sidelines. 10 analysts currently rate PLTR a Hold (i.e., Neutral), while 2 (including Jaluria) have Sell ratings, and 5 remain bullish with Buys. On balance, the consensus still leans modestly to the upside, with an average price target of $192.88, implying about 15% upside from current levels. (See PLTR stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: Trump Says 'There Can Be No Going Back' on Greenland
data.description:
President Trump has doubled down on acquiring Greenland ahead of his trip to Davos, Switzerland for the World Economic Forum Annual Meeting.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"As I expressed to everyone, very plainly, Greenland is imperative for National and World Security," he said in a Truth Social post. "There can be no going back — On that, everyone agrees!"

Trump Ramps Up Tariff Threats Ahead of Davos Talks

Trump added that he agreed to meet with "various parties" in Davos. In a separate post, he shared screenshots of a text conversation between himself and French President Emmanuel Macron, who proposed a G7 meeting after Davos with representatives from Ukraine, Denmark, Syria, and Russia in attendance.

This morning, Trump threatened to impose a 200% tariff on French wine and champagne in response to Macron's unwillingness to join the newly created "Board of Peace" on Gaza. Leaders from the European Union are set to meet on Thursday to discuss a response to Trump's tariff proposals.

data.title: Why Temu-Owner PDD Stock Has Fallen for Six Straight Sessions
data.description:
Shares of PDD Holdings (PDD), the China-based e-commerce firm, have fallen for six straight sessions, dropping more than 12% since January 8, as investors react to rising regulatory pressure in China.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

According to a Bloomberg report, Chinese authorities have widened a probe into the company following clashes between PDD employees and regulators last month. Reportedly, regulators have since sent more than 100 investigators to PDD's Shanghai headquarters to carry out on-site checks.

Regulatory Review Beyond Initial Checks

The investigation now covers a wide range of issues, including alleged fake deliveries, tax matters, and other compliance concerns. Regulators have carried out interviews and document reviews at the company's offices as part of the process.

The increased scrutiny is already affecting operations. Marketing plans ahead of the Lunar New Year have slowed, and some projects have been delayed as staff prepare for further inspections and interviews.

Is PDD a Good Stock to Buy Now?

PDD has grown quickly over the past few years, rising from a small startup into one of China's largest online shopping firms. It is now best known outside China as the owner of Temu, which competes with Shein in the U.S. and Europe.

That growth may now be drawing closer attention from regulators. While PDD has not commented on the probe, the growing regulatory scrutiny has weighed on sentiment. For now, investors remain cautious as they assess how much the investigation could disrupt operations and earnings.

Turning to Wall Street, PDD stock carries a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on seven Buys, four Holds, and zero Sell ratings. The average PDD stock price target is $146.39, implying 37% upside potential over the course of 2026.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: FAST Earnings: Fastenal Company Stock Unravels on Mixed Q4
data.description:
Fastenal Company's (FAST) shares sank about 4% at the start of trading on Tuesday. This came after the wholesale industrial and construction supply distributor delivered uneven fourth-quarter results, with earnings per share (EPS) in line but revenue coming in just shy of expectations.

  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During the three-month period ended December 31, Fastenal grew EPS by 13% from the year-ago quarter to 26 cents, matching analyst consensus. However, its revenue of $2.03 billion — which rose 12% from a year ago — missed the Wall Street consensus of $2.04 billion.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Sink as Trump Triggers Tariff Fears over Greenland
data.description:
U.S. stock futures plunged on Tuesday morning, starting the holiday-shortened trading week on a negative note. Investor sentiment was impacted by a new round of tariffs threatened by U.S. President Donald Trump over his demand to acquire Greenland, rattling global markets. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 1.65%, 1.35%, and 1.22%, respectively, at 8:30 a.m. EST on January 20.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

U.S. markets were closed on Monday in honor of the Martin Luther King Jr. holiday. Last week, all three major averages closed lower, despite the rally in semiconductor stocks following Taiwan Semiconductor's (TSM) blockbuster results. The S&P 500 and the Dow Jones fell 0.4% and 0.3%, respectively, while the Nasdaq Composite declined 0.7%.

In a Truth Social post, Trump announced a 10% U.S. tariff on eight European allies, including the UK, effective February 1. He added that tariffs will eventually rise to 25% on June 1 and will stay in place until the U.S. strikes a deal to acquire Greenland. Moreover, Trump threatened to slap a 200% tariff on French wines and champagne. Looking forward, investors will focus on Trump's speech at the World Economic Forum in Davos on Wednesday, where he will discuss the Greenland situation.

Meanwhile, investors await the fourth-quarter results of streaming giant Netflix (NFLX), scheduled to be announced after the market closes today.

Turning to trending stocks, shares of 3M (MMM), the maker of Post-it Notes and Scotch Tape, were down 5.5% in Tuesday's pre-market trading after the company reported mixed results for Q4 2025.

Also, Fifth Third Bancorp (FITB) stock was down almost 2% after the regional bank's net interest income slightly lagged expectations, though earnings beat estimates. Likewise, KeyCorp (KEY) stock was down 1.8% as the company's Q4 2025 results failed to impress investors. Micron Technology (MU) shares were down 1.5% in pre-market trading after the chip company signed a letter of intent to buy Powerchip Semiconductor Manufacturing Corporation's fab in Taiwan for $1.8 billion.

data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: SOUN Stock Slides — 3 Red Flags Investors Can't Ignore in 2026
data.description:
SoundHound AI (SOUN) shares fell about 3% in early trading on Tuesday. The stock has drawn strong investor interest amid rapid growth in voice AI and a series of high-profile partnerships. However, looking ahead to 2026, several risks are becoming harder to ignore. From ongoing profitability concerns to intensifying competition, investors may want to weigh these red flags carefully before betting on the stock's next move higher.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

So far in 2026, SOUN stock is up more than 11%, driven largely by fresh optimism following its latest product showcases and partnership announcements at CES 2026. Meanwhile, rising Wall Street attention and bullish analyst forecasts have further supported the stock's momentum into 2026.

Let's dig deeper.

1. Intense Competition

SoundHound AI creates voice technology that lets apps, devices, and cars understand and respond to people naturally. The voice AI market is highly competitive, with many players chasing the same customers as SoundHound, drawn by the industry's rapid growth. While the overall AI landscape is large enough to support multiple winners, execution will be critical. To earn lasting investor confidence, SOUN must demonstrate consistent revenue and earnings growth while steadily improving operational efficiency.

Looking ahead, SoundHound AI is scheduled to report its Q4 2025 earnings on February 26. The company expects full-year revenue to come in between $165 million and $180 million, nearly doubling from $84.7 million in 2024.

2. Valuation Concerns

SOUN's valuation remains a key risk. The stock trades at a steep price-to-sales multiple, signaling high growth expectations but leaving little margin for error. Any slowdown in execution or intensifying competition could pressure the stock.

Notably, SOUN's trailing 12-month price-to-sales ratio stands at 29.53, far above the sector average of 3.58.

3. Profitability Issues

SoundHound struggles with profitability as high operating costs outpace revenue growth, leading to persistent losses and negative cash flow. Net loss widened to $351.1 million in 2024, up 283% from 2023, largely due to a $222.6 million fair value adjustment from recent acquisitions.

In the latest quarter, adjusted net loss was $0.03 per share, better than analysts' $0.09 estimate and an improvement from $0.04 a year earlier.

What Is the Price Target for SOUN?

Despite these risks, SoundHound AI's long-term outlook remains positive, with analysts on Wall Street cautiously optimistic.

According to TipRanks, SOUN stock has received a Moderate Buy consensus rating, with five Buys and two Holds assigned in the last three months. SoundHound's average stock price target is $16.60, suggesting a potential upside of almost 50% from the current level.
data.author: Kirti Tak
